converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
National Historic Preservation Program oral histories
Glass, James Arthur, 1952-
4327
168 audiocassettes. 168 audiocassettes.
1986-1987. Interviews (59) by James A. Glass on the subject of the development of the National Historic Preservation Program between 1957 and 1980. Interviewees include Lawrence E. Aten, Edwin C. Bearss, Martha M. Bigelow, Tersh Boasberg, J.O. Brew, Helen D. Bullock, John A. Burns, James H. Charleton, S. Allen Chambers, Jr., Ernest Allen Connally, Chris T. Delaporte, Eric N. DeLony, Antoinette F. Downing, Carl Feiss, Robert G. Ferris, John Fowler, Russell W. Fridley, Robert R. Garvey, Jr., George B. Hartzog, Jr., Gary L. Hume, Casey and Lucille Ireland, H. Ward Jandl, George A. Karas, Russell V. Keune, Thomas F. King, Bennie C. Keel, Lawson B. Knott, Jr., James M. Lambe, Ben Levy, Nellie L. Longworth, Elizabeth A. Lyon, James C. Massey, John D. McDermott, Lee McElvain, Terry B. Morton, William Penn Mott, Jr., William J. Murtagh, Lee H. Nelson, Loretta Neumann, Stephen D. Newman, Sally G. Oldham, Mary Carolyn Pitts, Constance Werner Ramirez, Dwight F. Rettie, Jerry L. Rogers, Douglas H. Scovill, Horace J. Sheely, Jr., Carol D. Shull, Ann Webster Smith, Larry E. Tise, Merle Wells, Thomas B. Williams, Frederick C. Williamson, and Alfred B. Wolfe. Collection material in English Cassette tapes. 2100894
Access Restrictions:
Individual interviews may have specific restrictions.
Conditions Governing Access
Access to Ben Levy interview restricted until permission is granted to open.
Conditions Governing Access
Access to William J. Murtagh interview restricted during lifetime of interviewee or by his written permission.
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Oral history tapes were compiled as research for a Ph.D. dissertation.
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Staff
Date completed:
1987
EAD encoding:
Peter Martinez, May 2004Maria Keating
Date modified:
RMC Staff, December 2014
Cite As:
National Historic Preservation Program oral histories, #4327. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Interviews (59) by James A. Glass on the subject of the development of the National Historic Preservation Program between 1957 and 1980. Interviewees include Lawrence E. Aten, Edwin C. Bearss, Martha M. Bigelow, Tersh Boasberg, J.O. Brew, Helen D. Bullock, John A. Burns, James H. Charleton, S. Allen Chambers, Jr., Ernest Allen Connally, Chris T. Delaporte, Eric N. DeLony, Antoinette F. Downing, Carl Feiss, Robert G. Ferris, John Fowler, Russell W. Fridley, Robert R. Garvey, Jr., George B. Hartzog, Jr., Gary L. Hume, Casey and Lucille Ireland, H. Ward Jandl, George A. Karas, Russell V. Keune, Thomas F. King, Bennie C. Keel, Lawson B. Knott, Jr., James M. Lambe, Ben Levy, Nellie L. Longworth, Elizabeth A. Lyon, James C. Massey, John D. McDermott, Lee McElvain, Terry B. Morton, William Penn Mott, Jr., William J. Murtagh, Lee H. Nelson, Loretta Neumann, Stephen D. Newman, Sally G. Oldham, Mary Carolyn Pitts, Constance Werner Ramirez, Dwight F. Rettie, Jerry L. Rogers, Douglas H. Scovill, Horace J. Sheely, Jr., Carol D. Shull, Ann Webster Smith, Larry E. Tise, Merle Wells, Thomas B. Williams, Frederick C. Williamson, and Alfred B. Wolfe.
Subjects include the National Historic Preservation Act of 1966, the National Park Service, the National Trust for Historic Preservation, the Advisory Council on Historic Preservation, the National Conference of State Historic Preservation Officers, the Office of Archeology and Historic Preservation of the National Park Service, the Heritage Conservation and Recreation Service, Preservation Action, Inc., the U.S. Conference of Mayors' Special Committee on Historic Preservation (Rains Committee), the Tax Reform Act of 1976, Executive Order 11593 of 1971, and the 1980 amendments to the National Historic Preservation Act.
Restrictions on use:
Tersh Boasberg interview: citations and quotations require written permission until 2004 or during lifetime of interviewee.
Conditions Governing Use
Helen D. Bullock interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.|Russell V. Keune interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.
Conditions Governing Use
Nellie L. Longworth interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.
Conditions Governing Use
Lee McElvain interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.
Conditions Governing Use
Terry B. Morton interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.
Conditions Governing Use
William Penn Mott, Jr. interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.
Conditions Governing Use
Sally G. Oldham interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.
Conditions Governing Use
Constance Werner Ramirez interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.
Conditions Governing Use
Carol D. Shull interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.
Conditions Governing Use
Ann Webster Smith interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.
Conditions Governing Use
Larry E. Tise interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.
Conditions Governing Use
Thomas B. Williams interview: citations and quotation require written permission during lifetime of interview.
Conditions Governing Use
Albert B. Wolfe interview: citations and quotations require written permission of interviewee.
Conditions Governing Use
Elizabeth A. Lyon interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.
Architecture -- United States -- Conservation and restoration. Historic sites -- Law and legislation -- United States. Historic buildings -- United States -- Conservation and restoration. Aten, Lawrence E. Bearss, Edwin C. Bigelow, Martha M. Boasberg, Tersh. Brew, J. O. Bullock, Helen Claire Duprey, 1904- Burns, John A. Charleton, James H. Chambers, S. Allen. Connally, Ernest Allen Delaporte, Chris T. DeLony, Eric N. Downing, Antoinette Forrester. Feiss, Carl. Ferris, Robert G. Fowler, John. Fridley, Russell W. Garvey, Robert R. Hartzog, George B. Hume, Gary L. Ireland, Casey. Ireland, Lucille. Jandl, H. Ward. Karas, George A. Keune, Russell V. King, Thomas F. Keel, Bennie C., 1934- Knott, Lawson B. Lambe, James M. Levy, Ben. Longworth, Nellie L. Lyon, Elizabeth A. Massey, James C. McDermott, John D. McElvain, Lee. Morton, Terry B. Mott, William Penn. Murtagh, William J. Nelson, Lee H. Neumann, Loretta. Newman, Stephen D. Oldham, Sally G. Pitts, Mary Carolyn. Ramirez, Constance Werner. Rettie, Dwight Fay. Rogers, Jerry L. Scoville, Douglas H. Sheely, Horace J., Jr. Shull, Carol D. Smith, Ann Webster. Tise, Larry E. Wells, Merle. Williams, Thomas B. Williamson, Frederick B. Wolfe, Albert B. National Historic Preservation Program National Trust for Historic Preservation in the United States National Conference of State Historic Preservation Officers Preservation Action United States. National Park Service United States. Advisory Council on Historic Preservation United States. Office of Archeology and Historic Preservation United States. Heritage Conservation and Recreation Service United States Conference of Mayors. Special Committee on Historic Preservation United States. National Historic Preservation Act of 1966 United States. National Historic Preservation Act Amendments of 1980 United States. Tax Reform Act of 1976
